title: Dynamic Glazing Visible Light Transmittance Levels
summary: Dynamic architectural glazing typically provides four tint levels corresponding to specific visible light transmittance percentages.
evidence_quote: Typically, the tint corresponds to 4%, 20%, 40%, and 60% visible light transmittance Tvis depending on manufacturers.

title: Dynamic Glazing Application Feasibility
summary: Dynamic glazing is most feasible on large glazing facades that do not face north and works well on east and west orientations.
evidence_quote: Dynamic glazing is usually the most feasible on large glazing facades not do not face north. It works well on east and west orientations since these are difficult to horizontally shade.

title: Dynamic Glazing South Facade Application
summary: Dynamic glazing can also be used efficiently on south facades that are not shaded.
evidence_quote: It could also be used efficiently on south facades that are not shaded.

title: Dynamic Glazing Control Method
summary: Dynamic glazing is controlled by a low voltage current and can be operated manually or via a building management system.
evidence_quote: The dynamic glazing is usually controlled by a low voltage current and can be controlled either manually through a switch or linked into an automatic building management system (BMS).

title: Dynamic Glazing Energy Benefit
summary: Dynamic glazing reduces solar heat gain, which is the amount of heat entering a glass structure from the sun.
evidence_quote: reduces energy costs by reducing solar heat gain (i.e., the amount of heat that enters a glass structure naturally from the sun).

title: Desiccant Process Types
summary: There are two basic types of open-cycle desiccant processes: solid and liquid desiccant.
evidence_quote: There are two basic types of open-cycle desiccant process: solid and liquid desiccant.

title: Desiccant Moisture Absorption Principle
summary: Moisture absorption occurs because the desiccant has a lower surface vapor pressure than the air.
evidence_quote: The moisture absorption process is caused by desiccant having a lower surface vapor pressure than the air.

title: Desiccant Reactivation Process
summary: Desiccant must be redried by a heating process called reactivation or regeneration for solid types, or reconcentration for liquid types.
evidence_quote: So that desiccant can be reused, it has to be redried by a heating process generally called reactivation or regeneration (for solid types) or reconcentration (for liquid types).
